label: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 18

extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL Remco attacked solo from breakaway, took third stage win, extended KOM lead while KUSS Sepp maintained 17-second GC lead over VINGEGAARD Jonas.
tactical_patterns: KRON Andreas attacked EVENEPOEL in breakaway, indicating desperation moves against superior climbers backfire when rivals have KOM priority.
VLASOV Aleksandr attached to DENZ Nico wheel specifically for top-10 GC positioning, revealing reliance on others' pacing late-race.
LANDA Mikel targeting AYUSO Juan for best-Spaniard classification drove fourth-place GC battle independent of podium contest.
VINGEGAARD Jonas and ROGLIČ Primož paced KUSS Sepp final meters despite team hierarchy questions, demonstrating resolved leadership after earlier controversy.
MAS Enric gained seconds over LANDA at finish despite defensive stage, showing opportunistic finishing even without attacking.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: Soloed to third stage win from breakaway, extending KOM dominance with metronomic climbing when others attacked.
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: Maintained 17-second GC lead with teammates publicly pacing him to line, resolving prior team cohesion questions.
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: Backed down on final climb, accepting domestique role 17 seconds behind KUSS rather than contesting.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: Supported KUSS in final meters instead of seeking stage position, confirming team hierarchy realignment.
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: negative: Required DENZ wheel to defend top-10 rather than riding independently, suggesting compromised climbing form.
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: negative: Struggling while supposedly supporting AYUSO, indicating inability to perform domestique duties late in grand tour.
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: negative: Had own troubles defending fourth overall from LANDA despite team support, revealing vulnerability to sustained pressure.
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: Aggressively targeting AYUSO for best-Spaniard despite placing fourth overall, showing late-race motivation beyond GC placement.
rider_signals.MAS Enric: positive: Gained seconds at finish over rivals, demonstrating sharp sprint finish when defensive tactics prevail.
rider_signals.KRON Andreas: negative: Attacking EVENEPOEL from breakaway failed completely, exposing tactical naivety against rider with clear KOM objectives.
rider_signals.CARUSO Damiano: neutral: Nearest to EVENEPOEL but unable to contest, suggesting limited upside when breakaway contains superior climber.
rider_signals.POOLE Max: neutral: Near leaders in breakaway through descents but faded, indicating handling skills insufficient without climbing power.
